reason: Response B exhibits hallmarks of Claude Opus's distinctive style: elevated, literary prose with extended metaphors ("cartographer," "photograph taken in a single moment," "the whole film"), philosophical density, and an aphoristic quality ("You called it Tuesday"). Response A, while competent and warm, reads as more straightforward and formulaic — closer to a generic encouraging note than Opus's characteristically richer, more intellectually textured writing. The closing line of B ("The door has not closed; it has simply asked you to knock twice") is the kind of compressed, resonant phrasing that Opus tends to produce.
